world_description: A narrative environment focused on how governments, corporations, and institutions compete to control advanced artificial intelligence infrastructure, including compute resources, semiconductor supply chains, cloud platforms, and foundational models. The world tracks signals indicating shifts in technological sovereignty, regulatory control, and geopolitical alignment around AI capability.

excluded_domains: general startup funding unrelated to infrastructure

primary_tensions: Commercial AI development vs national security classification
Open compute access vs export control restrictions
Private AI labs vs state oversight
initial_assumptions: AI capability increasingly correlates with access to compute infrastructure
Compute supply chains may become geopolitically segmented
States may treat frontier AI companies as strategic national assets
AI infrastructure may be reclassified as critical national infrastructure
analyst_notes: This world monitors signals indicating a transition from globally integrated AI development toward regionally governed AI infrastructure regimes.
